board: Modern Forestry — App Feedback & Bug Tracker
source: Candle Club beta feedback (Facebook / iMessage / texts), reporters anonymized to User N

permissions.admin: info@theforestrystudio.com — full CRUD (edit, status, delete, merge)
permissions.public: add-only — anyone may submit a ticket; name is optional on new submissions

statusLegend.fixed: Corrected and build-verified; ships in the next TestFlight build
statusLegend.needs-qa: Change is live/committed; awaiting on-device confirmation

backendState.note: Shopify rejects the raw native scheme ('Invalid redirect_uri scheme'). Shopify now redirects to the HTTPS Laravel callback, which bridges back to the native scheme. The iOS app reads all of this from /auth/config at runtime, so no client change was needed.
backendState.socialSignIn: Facebook verified On; Google enabled and confirmed on the live hosted sheet.
backendState.iosRelease: Modern Forestry iOS 1.0.2 build 14 uploaded to App Store Connect, submitted for App Review with automatic release after approval, distributed to internal TestFlight testers, and submitted for external TestFlight Beta Review. Public TestFlight link is enabled but gated by Apple's external beta approval: https://testflight.apple.com/join/DXeVGThS

tickets.title: Sign-in times out / kicks you out before you can finish
tickets.detail: Login times out within seconds; the emailed one-time-code screen disappears before the code can be entered. Happened on iPhone and iPad, with either email or the Shop connection.
tickets.resolution: Three causes, all resolved. (1) App: removed the 20s watchdog that cancelled the interactive auth session (028d06a). (2) Backend: prod was missing SHOPIFY_CUSTOMER_ACCOUNT_CLIENT_ID so /auth/config reported not-configured — now set in Forge. (3) Redirect: Shopify rejected the native scheme; Laravel now exposes an HTTPS callback that bridges back (6614ad3). Live /auth/config verified configured=true.

tickets.title: Login pre-fills the wrong / old email
tickets.detail: Sign-in defaulted to a stale, incorrect email address the tester no longer uses, because the web session reused shared Safari/Shop cookies.
tickets.resolution: Switched the auth session to ephemeral / cookie-isolated (315039d) so the user always authenticates as themselves. VERIFIED ON DEVICE 2026-07-08: the hosted sign-in sheet's email field renders blank, no stale address. See ticket 13 for the trade-off this creates with social sign-in.

tickets.title: Rewards tab asks me to sign in again
tickets.detail: Profile stays logged in (photo, saved candles) but the Rewards tab prompts for a second login.
tickets.resolution: Cause: if the token lapsed mid-session (no foreground transition to trigger the existing refresh), authenticated loads 401'd — Rewards fell to its sign-in gate while Account still rendered a cached snapshot. Added CustomerSession.ensureFreshToken(): a no-op when the token is valid, silent refresh when near expiry, never signs the user out on failure (12bd4fb). VERIFIED ON DEVICE 2026-07-09: Rewards opened from Account without a second sign-in gate and showed the Candle Cash snapshot.

tickets.title: "Save to Wishlist" says unavailable, but the item actually saves
tickets.detail: Tapping save shows 'wishlist unavailable', yet the item appears on the account/wishlist page afterward.
tickets.resolution: The save succeeded (2xx) but a strict response decode threw, surfacing a false error. Now decodes best-effort so a successful save never reports failure (6c38aaf).

tickets.title: Saving quiz-recommended candles shows "could not be saved"
tickets.detail: At the end of the scent quiz, saving suggested candles returned an error message.
tickets.resolution: Same root cause and fix as ticket 4 — both run through the shared add-to-wishlist call (6c38aaf).

tickets.title: Quiz can't be taken without signing in
tickets.detail: Trying to take the scent quiz forces a sign-in, which then failed.
tickets.resolution: Near-term: login now works, which removes the wall. Longer-term: the quiz load AND submit endpoints both require a bearer token, so true anonymous play needs new anonymous quiz endpoints on the backend (only saving would then prompt sign-in).

tickets.title: Offer another way to log in besides Shopify credentials
tickets.detail: Login only presented Shopify credentials; testers asked for alternatives and found the hand-off confusing.
tickets.resolution: Google and Facebook social sign-in credentials are now configured in Shopify Customer Accounts. Facebook verified On. Google enabled and verified in ticket 12. These are genuine options (no decoy buttons); Shopify remains the secure identity backend.

tickets.title: Unclear whether to 'Sign in as Reviewer' or just browse
tickets.detail: The Reviewer Access panel confused testers about how to sign in.
tickets.resolution: Clarify copy and de-emphasize Reviewer Access for normal customers. It is an App Store review bypass and must remain reachable for Apple's reviewers, so it cannot simply be deleted.

tickets.title: Android version?
tickets.detail: Tester asked whether an Android app is planned.
tickets.resolution: Confirmed: no Android build exists anywhere (searched for Gradle/Kotlin/Flutter/React Native/Capacitor). iOS-only today. If prioritized, a cross-platform (Expo/React Native) rewrite is the likely path for v2.

tickets.title: Add a dedicated Sale / clearance section
tickets.detail: Tester suggested a specific section for sale items.
tickets.resolution: Added a 'Sale' shelf at the top of the Shop, keyed to the live sale-items collection, with a sale-red accent and a photo banner (a535e85).

tickets.title: Hide the Shopify login/branding as much as possible
tickets.detail: The hosted sign-in shows shopify.com, and Google's consent screen says "Continue to shopify.com" rather than Modern Forestry. We want the sign-in to feel like our brand, not a third-party hand-off.
tickets.resolution: MECHANISM CONFIRMED 2026-07-08 (Shopify docs + live discovery doc). The iOS title bar is drawn by ASWebAuthenticationSession as an anti-phishing safeguard, and Apple exposes no API to hide/rename/restyle it. FIX APPLIED: the Shopify customer-accounts domain was moved to account.theforestrystudio.com and the hosted sign-in uses Modern Forestry branding. VERIFIED ON DEVICE 2026-07-09: the sheet title shows account.theforestrystudio.com, the Modern Forestry logo is present, and Google/Facebook buttons render. Remaining platform limit: Shopify's hosted account flow can still show Shopify-owned labels such as Continue with Shop; fully removing Shopify would mean abandoning Customer Account OAuth and breaking the identity link to orders/rewards/Everbranch, so this is now as branded as the supported platform path allows.

tickets.title: QA: confirm Google appears beside Facebook on the live sign-in sheet
tickets.detail: Google social sign-in was enabled in Shopify after the credentials were saved; Facebook is verified On.
tickets.resolution: VERIFIED ON DEVICE 2026-07-08: both Google and Facebook buttons render on the live hosted sign-in sheet, alongside 'Continue with Shop' and email. Social sign-in QA closed.

tickets.title: Decide: ephemeral auth session vs. one-tap social sign-in
tickets.detail: The ephemeral (cookie-isolated) session that fixed the wrong-email prefill (ticket 2) also means the sign-in sheet cannot reuse an existing Safari Google/Facebook session — so social sign-in asks for credentials on a fresh login instead of one-tap.
tickets.resolution: Decision: keep the safer account-choice flow. Tokens persist, so an interactive login is a rare, one-time cost, and it guarantees the user always authenticates as themselves (the wrong-email bug actively blocked a tester). ADDITIONAL BENEFIT found 2026-07-08: the isolated sign-in session also suppresses Apple's system consent alert ("'modernforestry' Wants to Use 'shopify.com' to Sign In"), which only appears for cookie-sharing sessions — so this already removes one prominent Shopify mention (see ticket 11).

tickets.title: Confirm App Store Guideline 4.8 compliance now that Google/Facebook login is enabled
tickets.detail: Apple's Guideline 4.8 (Login Services) requires that an app using a third-party or social login service to set up/authenticate the primary account also offers an equivalent privacy-preserving option — one that limits data collection to name and email, lets the user keep the email private, and doesn't track them for advertising without consent.
tickets.resolution: The app previously shipped (v1.0 build 2) with no social login. Google and Facebook are now enabled on the Shopify-hosted sign-in sheet, which may draw 4.8 scrutiny on the next submission. The Shopify email one-time-code option very likely satisfies the 'equivalent option' requirement (email only, no ad tracking). Confirm before resubmitting; if Apple pushes back, the fallback is adding Sign in with Apple as a Shopify social provider (verify Shopify supports it) or leaning on the email-code path. CAUTION: adding Sign in with Apple interacts badly with ticket 15 — Apple 'Hide My Email' relay addresses guarantee a duplicate customer account.

tickets.title: Social sign-in can create a duplicate customer account when the provider email differs
tickets.detail: A customer with existing orders/Candle Cash under email A who taps 'Continue with Google/Facebook' where that provider carries email B will land in a SECOND Shopify customer record — separate order history and separate rewards balance.
tickets.resolution: VERIFIED (Shopify docs + community): Shopify keys customers by EMAIL, not by login method; social sign-in syncs only the email. Same email across Google/Facebook/email-code = same account (Shopify enforces unique emails and matches). Different email = new account. This is not app-fixable — account resolution is entirely Shopify-side by email. Levers: (1) Shopify's unique-email rule prevents same-email dupes; (2) Shopify admin customer MERGE tool cleans up duplicates (info@); (3) sign-in copy nudging customers to use their order email. CONFIRMED IN EVERBRANCH CODE 2026-07-08: the rewards chain is Shopify customer (per-email) -> MarketingProfile (resolved via shopify_customer_id / external_customer_id in ModernForestryMobileCustomerSessionService) -> CandleCashBalance (belongsTo marketing_profile_id). So a different-email duplicate gets a different MarketingProfile and therefore a SEPARATE / zero Candle Cash balance; same email stays unified. A duplicate loses rewards visibility, confirmed. MERGE PATH ANSWERED 2026-07-09 (Everbranch code read): a Shopify customer-merge does NOT reconcile the Everbranch profile or Candle Cash. (a) profileForIdentity resolves the MarketingProfile via MarketingProfileLink.source_id = Shopify customer ID, never by email. (b) Everbranch handles only customers/create, customers/update, customers/redact webhooks — NO customers/merge and NO customers/delete — so on a Shopify merge the survivor gets a customers/update (updates only its own profile) while the losing customer's MarketingProfileLink + Candle Cash are orphaned; nothing moves the balance to the survivor. (c) There is NO transfer/reassign-balance-between-profiles capability anywhere; the admin 'duplicate' flow in MarketingCustomersController only prevents a NEW duplicate at manual-create time (attach to an existing profile), it does not combine two existing profiles' balances. Cleanup today is therefore manual and lossy (operator hand-keys the orphaned balance onto the survivor via a Candle Cash adjustment). RECOMMENDED BACKEND FIX (not iOS): subscribe to Shopify's customers/merge webhook and reconcile — re-point the MarketingProfileLink to the surviving customer and move the CandleCashTransactions / credit the net balance. GOTCHA: Sign in with Apple 'Hide My Email' relay addresses (@privaterelay.appleid.com) are per-app-unique and will ALWAYS produce a duplicate; factor into ticket 14.

tickets.title: Release notes: Modern Forestry iOS 1.0.2 build 14
tickets.detail:
The 1.0.2 build packages the recent beta fixes and release automation:

- Sign-in no longer times out while the customer leaves the app to retrieve an email one-time code.
- Sign-in uses an isolated web session so it does not pre-fill a stale Safari/Shopify email identity.
- Rewards proactively refreshes the customer token before authenticated loads, preventing a second login prompt mid-session.
- Wishlist add/remove treats successful 2xx saves as successful even if the response shape changes, removing false "wishlist unavailable" errors.
- Shop now has a dedicated Sale shelf at the top, backed by the live sale-items collection.
- Product layouts were not aligned consistently in the app and are now aligned.
- Customer Account OAuth client ID and redirect URI are fetched from /auth/config at runtime instead of being hardcoded in the app binary.
- Facebook Post sharing opens the system browser so the user's Facebook session and composer are available, and copies the share link as a fallback.
- Facebook Story sharing lets FBSDK choose the best composer surface instead of forcing native-only mode.
- Instagram Story sharing sends the required Meta source_application value so Instagram accepts the pasteboard story image.
- If Instagram is not installed, the app shows the iOS share sheet with the rendered scent card instead of dumping the user into a dead browser path.
- Fastlane release automation now builds, uploads, distributes TestFlight, submits App Review, and records the Modern Forestry reviewer login without committing secrets.
tickets.resolution: App Store Connect status as of 2026-07-10: version 1.0.2 build 14 uploaded, processed, submitted for App Review, and configured for automatic live release after Apple approval. Internal TestFlight distribution is active. Public TestFlight group 'Public TestFlight' is attached to build 14 and the public link is enabled at https://testflight.apple.com/join/DXeVGThS, but random testers remain gated until Apple approves external Beta Review.
